evals.expected_output: Reads 'Ocado Weekly List' (generic items) and 'Ocado Master List' from Apple Notes, resolves each item to best-value product on Ocado, shows proposed basket with prices/unit prices/deal flags, waits for approval before adding anything to trolley
assertions: proposed basket is displayed before any item is added to the trolley
generic items (lowercase) are resolved to best-value product with unit price shown
specific items (capitalised) are matched exactly or flagged as unavailable
estimated total is shown in the proposed basket
user is asked to confirm or modify before trolley is touched

evals.prompt: can you fill my Ocado trolley from my grocery list?
evals.expected_output: Same as above — never adds to trolley without showing proposed basket first
assertions: proposed basket is displayed before any item is added to the trolley
skill triggers correctly from this phrasing (not just 'do my Ocado shop')
at least one emoji status indicator (✅ ⚡ 🎯 ⚠️ ❌ ❓) is used per basket line

evals.prompt: do my Ocado shop — I want the cheapest options this week
evals.expected_output: Reads generic list, picks lowest unit price per item (still applying quality floor), flags any deals on master list, shows basket before adding
assertions: proposed basket is displayed before any item is added to the trolley
unit prices are shown for generic items to justify cheapest selection
quality floor is applied (items with <10 reviews flagged or skipped)
deal alerts section appears if any master list items are on promotion
